title: nonatelia hildebrandtii
intro: Nonatelia hildebrandtii, also known as the 'Madagascar Olive Tree', is a plant species native to the island of Madagascar and belongs to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues due to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. Nonatelia hildebrandtii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or poultice in traditional medicine, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
